title: storageKeys.token renamed + appStorage.get hardened with try/catch
commit_message: feat: rename token storage key to ACCESS_TOKEN and add parse error guard

why_fully_cross_dependent.explanation: src/services/storage.ts is never a primary source in standalone or feature test files. Every test that references storageKeys or appStorage lives in cross-dependent suites because it crosses at least two files: utilities.js (reads/writes via appStorage) + storage.ts (defines the keys). No pure standalone or feature test has storage.ts in its sources[].
why_fully_cross_dependent.pattern: changed_file  →  only cross-dependent tests match  →  zero standalone, zero feature selected

CRITICAL.reason: describe() label contains changed symbol (storageKeys / appStorage) AND src/services/storage.ts is listed in sources[]

tests.test: isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns false when no token is stored
tests.why: sources=[utilities.js, storage.ts], symbols=[isUserLoggedIn, storageKeys] — both files touched

tests.test: isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns true after a token has been stored under storageKeys.token
tests.why: Same cross-dependency; appStorage.get path now wrapped in try/catch

tests.test: updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the access token to localStorage under the TOKEN key
tests.why: Token key was 'TOKEN', now 'ACCESS_TOKEN' — test assertion will FAIL

tests.test: updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the refresh token under the REFRESHTOKEN key
tests.why: Same set; secondary storageKey also verified in this describe

tests.test: updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the login time under the LOGGEDINTIME key
tests.why: Same set; covers the full token-details write path

tests.test: storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.stripePaymentDetails equals STRIPEPAYMENTDETAILS
tests.why: sources=[storage.ts, constants.ts]; describes storageKeys contract for payment screens

tests.test: storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.token equals TOKEN
tests.why: sources=[storage.ts]; asserts token key = 'TOKEN' — WILL FAIL after rename to 'ACCESS_TOKEN'

HIGH.reason: No other tests have src/services/storage.ts in their sources[] but are not yet CRITICAL
HIGH.note: This is what makes this scenario 'fully cross-dependent' — the changed file only appears in cross-dependent test sources

SKIP.reason: src/services/storage.ts does not appear in sources[] for any of these tests
skipped_files: test_repository/cross-dependent/auth-storage.cross.test.js  (validateEmailOrUsername, generateCardNumber, capitalizeFirstLetter describes — storage.ts not in their sources[])
test_repository/cross-dependent/payment-state.cross.test.js (updateUserCards, updatePaymentPlanDetails, CLEARCARDS, CARD_NUMBER_REGEX describes — storage.ts not in sources[])
test_repository/feature/user-profile.feature.test.js    (sources: userDetailsReducer.js, actiotypes.js only — storage logic inlined in test)

coverage_gaps.test: storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.token equals TOKEN
coverage_gaps.reason: Test asserts value is 'TOKEN' but diff renames it to 'ACCESS_TOKEN'

coverage_gaps.test: updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the access token to localStorage under the TOKEN key
coverage_gaps.reason: The key written to localStorage is now 'ACCESS_TOKEN' not 'TOKEN'
coverage_gaps.action_required: Update test to read from storageKeys.token which is now 'ACCESS_TOKEN'

coverage_gaps.reason: try/catch error-handling path in appStorage.get has no test — corrupted JSON in localStorage now returns null silently
recommended_tests: returns null when localStorage contains malformed JSON for the given key
does not throw when JSON.parse fails

jest_run_command: npx jest --config test_repository/jest.config.js --no-coverage -t "isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token|updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys|storageKeys align with payment flow expectations"
